name: Kindergarten Fishing & Water Skills — Semester 1
description: An 18-week, play-based Kindergarten pathway for water safety, fish observation, and shoreline stewardship. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.
learningGoals: Build confidence and curiosity with water safety, fish observation, and shoreline stewardship.
Communicate learning through talk, drawing, movement, play, or a simple creation.
Practice safe, kind, and responsible participation.

lessons.title: Notice and talk: water safety, fish observation, and shoreline stewardship
lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about water safety, fish observation, and shoreline stewardship through a close look at water safety, fish observation, and shoreline stewardship.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about water safety, fish observation, and shoreline stewardship through a guided practice with water safety, fish observation, and shoreline stewardship.

activities.title: A close look at water safety, fish observation, and shoreline stewardship
activities.learnerAction: With an adult or teacher nearby when needed, the learner participates in a close look at water safety, fish observation, and shoreline stewardship, uses words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate thinking, and cleans up safely.

successCriteria: The learner participates safely and respectfully in water safety, fish observation, and shoreline stewardship.
The learner communicates one observation, choice, or question using speech, drawing, movement, or a supported response.
